title: `HAVING FIRST` returns undeterministic result
description:
### What happens?

Hi,

The following test case returns an undeterministic result. When I run ten times, 7 of them return an empty result and the remaining return one row.

```
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 VARCHAR DEFAULT(DATE '1970-01-19'), c1 BIGINT DEFAULT(0.6662346215934555));
INSERT INTO t0(c0, c1) VALUES (811399017, 2045733589);
CREATE VIEW v0(c0) AS SELECT t0.c1 FROM t0 WHERE t0.rowid GROUP BY t0.c1;
INSERT INTO t0(c0, c1) VALUES (-1719245904, DEFAULT);
INSERT INTO t0(c1) VALUES (-268870676);
SELECT true FROM v0 WHERE v0.c0 HAVING FIRST((v0.c0 BETWEEN false AND v0.c0));
```

If I remove the `DEFAULT(0.6662346215934555)`, the test case will always return empty result.

### To Reproduce

```
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 VARCHAR DEFAULT(DATE '1970-01-19'), c1 BIGINT DEFAULT(0.6662346215934555));
INSERT INTO t0(c0, c1) VALUES (811399017, 2045733589);
CREATE VIEW v0(c0) AS SELECT t0.c1 FROM t0 WHERE t0.rowid GROUP BY t0.c1;
INSERT INTO t0(c0, c1) VALUES (-1719245904, DEFAULT);
INSERT INTO t0(c1) VALUES (-268870676);
SELECT true FROM v0 WHERE v0.c0 HAVING FIRST((v0.c0 BETWEEN false AND v0.c0));
```

### OS:

Ubuntu

### DuckDB Version:

1.3.1

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Chi Zhang

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1(c0 INTEGER, c1 FLOAT DEFAULT(1.5), c2 TEXT DEFAULT('default'));
INSERT INTO t1(c0, c1) VALUES (1, NULL);
CREATE VIEW v1 AS SELECT c1 FROM t1 WHERE c0 IS NOT NULL GROUP BY c1;
SELECT true FROM v1 WHERE (v1.c1 IS NOT NULL AND v1.c1 > 2);
